concepts.name: expected prediction error
concepts.description: Expected value of squared prediction error over an independent test sample, measuring how well a model generalizes.
concepts.quote: Test error, also referred to as generalization error, is the prediction error over an independent test sample

concepts.name: training error
concepts.description: Average loss over the training sample, typically an optimistic estimate of test error.
concepts.quote: Training error is the sample analogue

concepts.name: test error
concepts.description: Prediction error over an independent test sample drawn from the same population as the training data.
concepts.quote: Test error, also referred to as generalization error, is the prediction error over an independent test sample

concepts.name: loss function
concepts.description: Function L(Y, f(X)) that penalizes prediction errors, measuring cost of predicting f(X) when true value is Y.
concepts.quote: To proceed, we need a loss function L(Y, f(X)) for penalizing errors in prediction

concepts.name: squared error loss
concepts.description: Loss function L(Y, f(X)) = (Y - f(X))², the most common choice for quantitative responses.
concepts.quote: By far the most common and convenient is squared error loss: L(Y, f(X)) = (Y − f(X))²

concepts.name: absolute error loss
concepts.description: Loss function L(Y, f(X)) = |Y - f(X)|, less sensitive to outliers than squared error.
concepts.quote: Absolute error loss L(Y, f(X)) = |Y − f(X)|

concepts.name: 0-1 loss
concepts.description: Loss function for classification: L(G, Ĝ(X)) = I(G ≠ Ĝ(X)), assigns 1 for errors and 0 for correct predictions.
concepts.quote: For categorical outputs, we can use the 0-1 loss function

concepts.name: conditional expectation
concepts.description: E[Y|X=x], the expected value of Y given X=x, which minimizes expected squared error loss.
concepts.quote: the conditional expectation, also known as the regression function

concepts.name: conditional median
concepts.description: The median of Y given X=x, which minimizes expected absolute error loss.
concepts.quote: The conditional median is the optimal predictor under absolute error loss

concepts.name: bayes classifier
concepts.description: The classifier that assigns each point to the most probable class given X, minimizing expected 0-1 loss.
concepts.quote: This reasonable solution is known as the Bayes classifier, and says that we classify to the most probable class

concepts.name: bayes decision boundary
concepts.description: The boundary between regions where the Bayes classifier assigns different classes.
concepts.quote: Figure 2.5 shows the Bayes-optimal decision boundary

concepts.name: least squares fitting
concepts.description: Method for fitting linear models by minimizing residual sum of squares.
concepts.quote: we fit this model by least squares

concepts.name: residual sum of squares
concepts.description: RSS(β) = Σ(yi - xᵢᵀβ)², the criterion minimized in least squares fitting.
concepts.quote: Residual sum of squares

concepts.name: fitted values
concepts.description: Predictions ŷ = Xβ̂ from a fitted linear model on the training data.
concepts.quote: The fitted values at the training inputs are

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor averaging
concepts.description: Method that predicts by averaging the response values of the k closest training points.
concepts.quote: The k-nearest neighbor fit for Ŷ is defined as averaging over the k nearest neighbors

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor classifier
concepts.description: Classification method assigning to the majority class among k nearest training neighbors.
concepts.quote: k-nearest neighbor classifier

concepts.name: nearest neighbor decision boundary
concepts.description: The piecewise linear or irregular boundary formed by nearest neighbor classification.
concepts.quote: The decision boundary is irregular

concepts.name: curse of dimensionality
concepts.description: Phenomenon where nearest neighbors become far away in high dimensions, requiring exponentially more data for local methods.
concepts.quote: in high dimensions all feasible training samples sparsely populate the input space

concepts.name: sampling density
concepts.description: Density of observations per unit volume in input space, which decreases exponentially with dimension.
concepts.quote: To capture 1% or 10% of the data to form a local average requires a much larger neighborhood in high dimensions

concepts.name: empty space phenomenon
concepts.description: In high dimensions, most of the volume of a hypercube is near the boundaries, leaving the interior nearly empty.
concepts.quote: most data points are closer to a boundary than to any other data point

concepts.name: structured regression model
concepts.description: Regression model imposing constraints on the functional form to reduce complexity and avoid overfitting.
concepts.quote: We must make some assumptions about the structure of the regression function

concepts.name: linear model assumption
concepts.description: Assumption that regression function is linear in inputs: f(X) = Xᵀβ.
concepts.quote: The linear model makes huge assumptions about structure

concepts.name: additive model
concepts.description: Model where f(X) = Σfⱼ(Xⱼ), a sum of univariate functions of each predictor.
concepts.quote: An additive model assumes the regression function has the form

concepts.name: linear combination model
concepts.description: Model where f(X) = g(αᵀX), applying a function to a linear combination of inputs.
concepts.quote: linear combination model

concepts.name: interaction term
concepts.description: Product or other combination of predictors, allowing departure from pure additivity.
concepts.quote: We can include interaction terms and other derived features

concepts.name: roughness penalty
concepts.description: Penalty term J(f) added to RSS to discourage overly complex or wiggly functions.
concepts.quote: Here we augment the residual sum of squares with a roughness penalty

concepts.name: penalty functional
concepts.description: Functional J(f) measuring function complexity, large for rapidly varying functions.
concepts.quote: The user-selected functional J(f) will be large for functions f that vary too rapidly

concepts.name: smoothing parameter
concepts.description: Parameter λ controlling tradeoff between fit to data and roughness penalty.
concepts.quote: The smoothing parameter λ establishes the tradeoff between the residual sum of squares and the penalty

concepts.name: kernel function
concepts.description: Weighting function Kλ(x₀, x) assigning weights to observations based on distance from target point.
concepts.quote: These methods specify the nature of the local neighborhood explicitly by using a kernel weighting function

concepts.name: kernel width
concepts.description: Parameter controlling the size of the neighborhood in kernel methods, analogous to k in k-NN.
concepts.quote: The width of the kernel function is a smoothing parameter

concepts.name: nadaraya-watson estimator
concepts.description: Kernel-weighted average for regression: f̂(x) = Σ Kλ(x, xᵢ)yᵢ / Σ Kλ(x, xᵢ).
concepts.quote: The Nadaraya-Watson weighted average

concepts.name: local linear regression
concepts.description: Fits linear regression locally in a neighborhood, reducing bias compared to local averaging.
concepts.quote: local linear regression

concepts.name: locally weighted regression
concepts.description: Regression fit by weighted least squares with kernel weights based on distance from target point.
concepts.quote: locally weighted least squares

concepts.name: basis function expansion
concepts.description: Representing f(X) = Σ hₘ(X)βₘ as linear combination of fixed basis functions hₘ.
concepts.quote: represent f as a linear expansion of basis functions

concepts.name: radial basis function
concepts.description: Basis function hₘ(x) = D(||x - μₘ||) depending only on distance from center μₘ.
concepts.quote: radial basis functions

concepts.name: sigmoid basis function
concepts.description: Basis function hₘ(x) = σ(αₘᵀx + bₘ) using sigmoid activation, as in neural networks.
concepts.quote: sigmoid basis functions

concepts.name: dictionary
concepts.description: Collection D of candidate basis functions from which to build a model.
concepts.quote: dictionary D of candidate basis functions

concepts.name: bias-variance decomposition
concepts.description: Decomposition of expected prediction error into squared bias, variance, and irreducible error.
concepts.quote: the expected prediction error can be decomposed as bias squared plus variance

concepts.name: model complexity
concepts.description: Measure of flexibility of a model class, affecting bias-variance tradeoff.
concepts.quote: As we increase the model complexity, the variance tends to increase and the squared bias tends to decrease

concepts.name: degrees of freedom
concepts.description: Effective number of parameters in a model, measuring model complexity.
concepts.quote: degrees of freedom

concepts.name: irreducible error
concepts.description: Error due to intrinsic variability in Y that cannot be reduced by any prediction method.
concepts.quote: irreducible error

concepts.name: optimism of training error
concepts.description: Tendency of training error to underestimate true test error, increasing with model complexity.
concepts.quote: Training error tends to decrease as model complexity increases, but test error does not

concepts.name: in-sample error
concepts.description: Error measured on the training sample itself.
concepts.quote: in-sample error

concepts.name: out-of-sample error
concepts.description: Error measured on data not used for training.
concepts.quote: out-of-sample error

concepts.name: expected test error
concepts.description: The expected value of test error over all possible training sets of fixed size.
concepts.quote: the expected test error (also known as EPE)

concepts.name: pointwise prediction error
concepts.description: Prediction error at a specific point x₀ in the input space.
concepts.quote: error at a specific point x₀

concepts.name: misclassification error
concepts.description: Probability of incorrect classification, equivalent to expected 0-1 loss.
concepts.quote: the probability of misclassification

concepts.name: posterior probability
concepts.description: Pr(G=k|X=x), probability of class k given input x.
concepts.quote: Pr(G=k|X=x) is the conditional probability of class k

concepts.name: nearest neighbor region
concepts.description: Set of points closer to a given training point than to any other training point, forming Voronoi tessellation.
concepts.quote: the nearest neighbor region

concepts.name: euclidean distance
concepts.description: Standard distance metric ||x-x'|| used in k-nearest neighbors.
concepts.quote: using Euclidean distance

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor boundary
concepts.description: Decision boundary formed by k-NN classifier, which becomes smoother as k increases.
concepts.quote: The decision boundary is irregular with k=1 but becomes smoother as k increases

concepts.name: dimension reduction
concepts.description: Techniques to reduce the dimensionality of the input space before applying prediction methods.
concepts.quote: dimension reduction techniques

concepts.name: feature extraction
concepts.description: Creating new features as combinations or transformations of original variables.
concepts.quote: feature extraction

concepts.name: projection method
concepts.description: Method that projects high-dimensional data onto lower-dimensional subspace.
concepts.quote: projection methods

concepts.name: least squares projection
concepts.description: Fitting by least squares viewed as projection onto a linear subspace.
concepts.quote: least squares is a projection method

concepts.name: linear smoother
concepts.description: Smoother where fitted values are linear function of response: ŷ = Sy.
concepts.quote: linear smoothers

concepts.name: hat matrix
concepts.description: Matrix S such that ŷ = Sy in linear smoothers.
concepts.quote: the hat matrix

concepts.name: effective degrees of freedom
concepts.description: Trace of the hat matrix tr(S), measuring effective number of parameters.
concepts.quote: effective degrees of freedom tr(S)

concepts.name: model selection criterion
concepts.description: Criterion for choosing among models, balancing fit and complexity.
concepts.quote: model selection criterion

concepts.name: training set validation
concepts.description: Using held-out data to estimate test error and select models.
concepts.quote: validation

concepts.name: cross-validation error
concepts.description: Estimate of test error obtained by cross-validation.
concepts.quote: cross-validation

concepts.name: prior distribution
concepts.description: Distribution over function parameters representing beliefs before seeing data.
concepts.quote: prior distribution

concepts.name: posterior distribution
concepts.description: Distribution over parameters after conditioning on observed data.
concepts.quote: posterior distribution

concepts.name: bayesian framework
concepts.description: Approach treating parameters as random and computing posterior distributions.
concepts.quote: Bayesian methods

concepts.name: single hidden layer neural network
concepts.description: Neural network with one layer of hidden units between inputs and outputs.
concepts.quote: single hidden layer neural network

concepts.name: weight decay
concepts.description: Regularization for neural networks penalizing large weights.
concepts.quote: weight decay

concepts.name: squared bias
concepts.description: Component of prediction error equal to squared difference between expected prediction and true function, decreases with model complexity.
concepts.quote: the squared bias tends to decrease

concepts.name: variance of prediction
concepts.description: Component of prediction error measuring variability of predictions across training sets, increases with model complexity.
concepts.quote: As we increase the model complexity, the variance tends to increase

concepts.name: bayes rate
concepts.description: The error rate of the Bayes classifier, representing the irreducible error for a classification problem.
concepts.quote: The error rate of the Bayes classifier is called the Bayes rate

concepts.name: generalization error
concepts.description: Another term for test error, the error rate on new unseen data from the same distribution.
concepts.quote: Test error, also referred to as generalization error

concepts.name: regression function
concepts.description: Another name for conditional expectation f(x) = E[Y|X=x], the optimal predictor under squared error loss.
concepts.quote: the conditional expectation, also known as the regression function

concepts.name: activation function
concepts.description: Nonlinear function σ(x) applied to linear combinations in neural networks, typically sigmoid or ReLU.
concepts.quote: activation function σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x)

relationships.quote: the conditional expectation minimizes expected squared error loss
the conditional median minimizes expected absolute error loss
the Bayes classifier minimizes expected 0-1 loss
the Bayes rate is the error rate of the Bayes classifier
least squares fitting minimizes the residual sum of squares
k-nearest neighbor methods fail in high dimensions due to the curse of dimensionality
linear models avoid the curse of dimensionality by making strong structural assumptions
increasing model complexity affects the bias-variance tradeoff
variance increases with model complexity
squared bias decreases with model complexity
roughness penalty methods use a penalty functional to measure function complexity
the smoothing parameter controls the strength of the roughness penalty
kernel methods generalize k-nearest neighbors by using continuous weights
local linear regression generalizes Nadaraya-Watson by fitting local linear models
basis function expansions generalize linear models by using nonlinear transformations
radial basis functions are a type of basis function expansion
sigmoid basis functions are a type of basis function expansion
additive models generalize linear models by allowing nonlinear univariate functions
training error underestimates test error due to overfitting
optimism increases with model complexity
generalization error is another term for test error
expected prediction error can be decomposed into bias squared plus variance plus irreducible error
structured models impose constraints to avoid the curse of dimensionality
linear combination models are a type of structured regression model
interaction terms allow departure from pure additivity
squared error is the most common loss function for regression
absolute error is an alternative loss function for regression
0-1 loss is the standard loss function for classification
the Bayes classifier defines a decision boundary
k-nearest neighbor classifier produces an irregular decision boundary
locally weighted regression uses kernel weights
degrees of freedom is a measure of model complexity
for classification, irreducible error is equivalent to the Bayes rate
in-sample error is measured on the training sample
out-of-sample error is measured on new data
a loss function must be defined to compute expected prediction error
understanding conditional expectation is prerequisite to linear models
residual sum of squares is the criterion used in least squares
kernel methods require specifying a kernel function and its width
dictionary methods require a dictionary of basis functions
expected test error averages over all possible training sets
expected prediction error averages pointwise error over input distribution
regression function is another name for conditional expectation
Bayes classifier requires computing posterior probabilities
misclassification error is expected 0-1 loss
k-NN classifier defines a decision boundary
k-NN requires a distance metric, typically Euclidean
nearest neighbor methods partition space into regions
dimension reduction helps overcome curse of dimensionality
feature extraction is a type of dimension reduction
projection methods are a type of dimension reduction
least squares can be viewed as a projection operation
linear smoothers are characterized by a hat matrix
effective degrees of freedom is the trace of the hat matrix
linear smoothers include least squares as a special case
model selection criteria balance bias and variance
cross-validation estimates test error
validation is a model selection approach
posterior distribution combines prior with observed data
Bayesian methods can be viewed as roughness penalties with priors
Bayesian methods require specifying a prior distribution
neural networks use activation functions for nonlinearity
weight decay is a form of penalty on complexity
neural networks use sigmoid activation functions
neural networks are adaptive basis function expansions
effective degrees of freedom generalizes the parameter count
effective degrees of freedom measures model complexity
model selection aims to minimize expected prediction error
optimism is the gap between test and training error
